content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vuongluu/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec review loop** — dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent with precisely crafted review context (never your session history); fix issues and re-dispatch until approved (max 3 iterations, then surface to human)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec review loop" [shape=box];
    "Spec review passed?" [shape=diamond];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec review loop";
    "Spec review loop" -> "Spec review passed?";
    "Spec review passed?" -> "Spec review loop" [label="issues found,\nfix and re-dispatch"];
    "Spec review passed?" -> "User reviews spec?" [label="approved"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Review Loop:**
After writing the spec document:

1. Dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent (see spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md)
2. If Issues Found: fix, re-dispatch, repeat until Approved
3. If loop exceeds 3 iterations, surface to human for guidance

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me compile everything:

## Comprehensive Blockchain Chain Integration Analysis - ClawQuest

Based on my thorough exploration of the ClawQuest monorepo, here's a detailed analysis of how blockchain chains are currently integrated:

---

### 1. SUPPORTED CHAINS CONFIGURATION

**Primary Chain Registry**: `/Users/vuongluu/Documents/jobs/lab3/ceo/clawquest/packages/shared/src/chains.ts`

**Currently Supported Chains**:
- **Base** (Chain ID: 8453) - Mainnet
- **Base Sepolia** (Chain ID: 84532) - Testnet ✓ DEFAULT
- **BNB Smart Chain (BSC)** (Chain ID: 56) - Mainnet
- **BSC Testnet** (Chain ID: 97) - Testnet
- **Ethereum** (Chain ID: 1) - Mainnet (reference only)
- **Arbitrum** (Chain ID: 42161) - Mainnet (reference only)
- **Polygon** (Chain ID: 137) - Mainnet (reference only)

**Escrow-Enabled Chains** (actively used):
- `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS = [8453, 84532, 56, 97]` (Base + BSC)

**XLayer Support**: Currently only mapped in OKX API integration (onchain.service.ts) for wallet portfolio queries with chain ID 196, but NOT in escrow or core chain config.

---

### 2. TOKEN CONFIGURATION BY CHAIN

**Token Registry**: `/packages/shared/src/chains.ts` lines 88-130

**Token Addresses**:

| Chain | Token | Address | Decimals |
|-------|-------|---------|----------|
| Base (8453) | USDC | 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913 | 6 |
| Base (8453) | USDT | 0xfde4C96c8593536E31F229EA8f37b2ADa2699bb2 | 18 |
| Base (8453) | ETH (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| Base Sepolia (84532) | USDC | 0xAA87A3f8E6017F1f02af0f306B765FCfBeCac3E4 | 6 |
| Base Sepolia (84532) | ETH (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| BNB (56) | USDC | 0x8AC76a51cc950d9822D68b83fE1Ad97B32Cd580d | 18 |
| BNB (56) | USDT | 0x55d398326f99059fF775485246999027B3197955 | 18 |
| BNB (56) | BNB (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| BSC Testnet (97) | USDT | 0x337610d27c682E347C9cD60BD4b3b107C9d34dDd | 18 |
| BSC Testnet (97) | tBNB (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| Polygon (137) | USDC | 0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359 | 6 |
| Polygon (137) | USDT | 0xc2132D05D31c914a87C6611C10748AEb04B58e8F | 6 |

**Token Helper Functions**:
- `getTokenInfo(chainId, symbol)` - Get token by chain + symbol
- `getTokenByAddress(chainId, address)` - Get token by chain + contract address
- `isNativeToken(address)` - Check if address is native token (0x0000...)

---

### 3. SMART CONTRACT DEPLOYMENT

**Escrow Contract**: `/Users/vuongluu/Documents/jobs/lab3/ceo/clawquest/contracts/src/ClawQuestEscrow.sol`

**Key Features**:
- UUPS Upgradeable proxy pattern
- Single instance per chain
- Role-based access control (OPERATOR_ROLE, PAUSER_ROLE, UPGRADER_ROLE)
- Token allowlist (configurable per chain)
- Multi-sponsor deposit model with sub-questIds

**Deploy Scripts**:
1. `/contracts/script/Deploy.s.sol` - Standard deployment
2. `/contracts/script/DeployCreate2.s.sol` - Deterministic CREATE2 (same address across chains)

**Foundry Configuration**: `/contracts/foundry.toml`
```toml
[rpc_endpoints]
base_sepolia = "https://sepolia.base.org"
base = "https://mainnet.base.org"
bnb = "https://bsc-dataseed.binance.org"
bsc_testnet = "https://data-seed-prebsc-1-s1.binance.org:8545"

[etherscan]
base_sepolia = { key = "${BASESCAN_API_KEY}" }
base = { key = "${BASESCAN_API_KEY}" }
bnb = { key = "${BSCSCAN_API_KEY}" }
bsc_testnet = { key = "${BSCSCAN_API_KEY}" }
```

**Contract Deployment Configuration**:
```env
# Base Sepolia (testnet) — LIVE contract
REDACTED

# Per-chain contract addresses (optional):
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_8453=0x...    (Base mainnet)
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_97=0x...      (BSC Testnet)

# Shared CREATE2 address (fallback for all chains):
# ESCROW_CONTRACT=0x...
```

---

### 4. ESCROW/FUNDING SYSTEM

**Escrow Configuration Module**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts`

**Active Chains Determination**:
1. `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` env var (comma-separated) → validated against SUPPORTED_CHAINS
2. Fallback: `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` constant filtered by `ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE` (testnet|mainnet)
3. Default chain: `ESCROW_CHAIN_ID` env var (falls back to first active chain)

**RPC URL Resolution Priority**:
1. `RPC_URL_<chainId>` env vars (canonical format)
2. Legacy named env vars: `RPC_URL_BASE`, `RPC_URL_BASE_SEPOLIA`, `RPC_URL_BNB`, `RPC_URL_BSC_TESTNET`
3. Fallback: Public RPC from `SUPPORTED_CHAINS`

**RPC Manager Service**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/rpc-manager.service.ts`
- Loads active RPCs from database (`ChainRpc` table)
- Health tracking with 5-minute cooldown after 3 failures
- Fallback to escrow.config if DB RPCs unavailable
- Multiple RPCs per chain supported with priority-based selection

**Escrow Client**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts`
- Uses viem for Ethereum interactions
- Creates public and wallet clients per chain
- Supports fallback transports across multiple RPCs
- Chain mapping for known chains (Base, BSC, Ethereum, etc.)
- Dynamic chain definition from SUPPORTED_CHAINS for unknown chains

**Escrow Routes**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.routes.ts`
- `GET /escrow/supported-chains` - Returns active chains for environment
- `GET /escrow/deposit-params/:questId` - Frontend parameters for deposit transactions
- Distribution and refund operations for operators

---

### 5. DATABASE SCHEMA

**Chain-Related Models**: `/apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`

```prisma
model Chain {
  id              Int              @id
  name            String
  shortName       String
  nativeCurrency  Json
  explorerUrl     String
  isTestnet       Boolean          @default(false)
  isEscrowEnabled Boolean          @default(false)
  isActive        Boolean          @default(true)
  createdAt       DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime         @updatedAt
  rpcs            ChainRpc[]
  contracts       EscrowContract[]
}

model ChainRpc {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  chainId   Int
  url       String
  provider  String?
  priority  Int      @default(10)
  isActive  Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
  chain     Chain    @relation(fields: [chainId])
  @@unique([chainId, url])
  @@index([chainId, isActive, priority])
}

model EscrowContract {
  id         String    @id @default(uuid())
  chainId    Int
  address    String
  isActive   Boolean   @default(true)
  deployedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime  @updatedAt
  chain      Chain     @relation(fields: [chainId])
  @@unique([chainId, address])
  @@index([chainId, isActive])
}

model EscrowCursor {
  chainId   Int      @id
  lastBlock BigInt
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}

model Quest {
  // ... other fields ...
  cryptoChainId        Int?
  cryptoTxHash         String?
  fundedAmount         Int?
  fundedAt             DateTime?
  fundingMethod        String?       // "stripe" or "crypto"
  fundingStatus        String        // "unfunded", "pending", "partial", "confirmed"
  tokenAddress         String?
  tokenDecimals        Int?
  tokenProvider        String?       // for USDT vs USDC selection
  network              String?       // e.g. "Base", "BNB Smart Chain"
  totalFunded          Decimal       @db.Decimal(36, 18)
  refundAmount         Int?
  refundStatus         String?
  refundTxHash         String?
  refundedAt           DateTime?
  escrowQuestId        String?       // quest ID for on-chain escrow
}

model QuestDeposit {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  questId       String
  escrowQuestId String
  userId        String
  amount        Decimal  @db.Decimal(36, 18)
  tokenAddress  String
  chainId       Int
  txHash        String?
  walletAddress String   @default("")
  status        String   // "pending", "confirmed", "refunded"
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  quest         Quest    @relation(fields: [questId])
  user          User     @relation(fields: [userId])
}

model WalletLink {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  address   String
  chainId   Int?      // optional: link to specific chain
  isPrimary Boolean   @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
  user      User      @relation(fields: [userId])
  @@unique([userId, address])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([address])
}
```

**Key Migration**: `/apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260305085350_chain_rpc/migration.sql`
- Adds Chain, ChainRpc, and EscrowContract tables
- Enables multi-RPC fallback per chain
- Supports multiple contract addresses per chain

---

### 6. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

**Core Chain Configuration** (`.env.example`):
```env
# Network mode (testnet = Base Sepolia + BSC Testnet, mainnet = Base + BNB)
ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE=testnet                # testnet | mainnet
VITE_ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE=testnet           # frontend counterpart

# Active chains (comma-separated chain IDs, source of truth)
ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS=84532                  # add chains: 84532,8453,56
ESCROW_CHAIN_ID=84532                   # default chain (84532=Base Sepolia, 8453=Base)

# Contract addresses
REDACTED
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_97=0x...      (BSC Testnet)
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_8453=0x...    (Base mainnet)
ESCROW_CONTRACT=""                      # Shared CREATE2 fallback

# Operator key
# OPERATOR_PRIVATE_KEY="0x..."

# Escrow poller
ESCROW_POLL_INTERVAL=15000              # ms between polls (default 15s)
ESCROW_CONFIRMATION_BLOCKS=5            # blocks behind tip (default 5)

# Custom RPC URLs (optional — defaults to public endpoints)
# RPC_URL_8453=https://...              # canonical format
# RPC_URL_84532=https://...
# RPC_URL_BNB=https://...               # legacy format
# RPC_URL_BSC_TESTNET=https://...
```

---

### 7. FRONTEND INTEGRATION

**Wagmi Configuration**: `/apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`
- Uses RainbowKit with viem
- Chains determined by `VITE_ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE`
- Testnet: `[baseSepolia, bscTestnet]`
- Mainnet: `[base, bsc]`
- Requires `VITE_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID`

**Escrow Routes**: `/apps/dashboard/src/routes/_authenticated/quests/$questId/fund.tsx`
- Handles deposit flow with chain/token selection
- Creates pending QuestDeposit records
- Integrates with wagmi for wallet connection

---

### 8. API ENDPOINTS

**Escrow Routes** (`/escrow` prefix):
- `GET /supported-chains` - List active chains and network mode
- `GET /deposit-params/:questId` - Params for deposit tx (questId, contract, token, chain)
- `POST /distribute/:questId` - Operator-only: distribute rewards
- `POST /refund/:questId` - Operator-only: refund deposits
- `GET /status/:questId` - Read on-chain escrow status

**Onchain Routes** (`/onchain` prefix):
- Portfolio queries via OKX API
- Supports chains: Ethereum, BSC, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, XLayer (196), Solana, Optimism, Avalanche

---

### 9. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Multi-Chain Design**:
1. **Shared Constants** (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`) - Single source of truth for chain metadata
2. **Per-Chain Contracts** - Different escrow contract per chain (CREATE2 for determinism)
3. **Per-Chain RPCs** - Database-backed RPC management with health tracking
4. **Sub-QuestIds** - Multi-sponsor deposits use keccak256(questId + sponsorId)

**Amount Handling**:
- Human-readable amounts in API/DB (e.g., 500 USDC)
- Convert to smallest unit on-chain using token decimals
- Helper functions: `toSmallestUnit()`, `fromSmallestUnit()`

**UUID → bytes32 Conversion**:
- Databases use UUIDs, smart contracts use bytes32
- `/packages/shared/src/escrow-utils.ts` handles conversion
- Quest ID: left-pad to 64 hex chars
- Sub-quest ID: keccak256(questIdBytes32 + sponsorIdBytes32)

---

### 10. MISSING/INCOMPLETE FEATURES

1. **XLayer (Chain ID 196)**: Currently only in portfolio mapping, not in escrow.config or token registry
2. **Multiple Tokens per Chain**: Token selection logic exists but not fully exposed in UI
3. **Mainnet Deployment**: Base (8453) and BNB (56) contracts not yet configured in .env
4. **Chain Management API**: No admin endpoints to dynamically add/remove chains at runtime
5. **Polygon/Arbitrum/Ethereum**: Defined in SUPPORTED_CHAINS but no deployed contracts

---

### 11. DEPLOYMENT CHECKLIST FOR NEW CHAINS

To add a new chain (e.g., XLayer 196):

1. **Add to SUPPORTED_CHAINS** (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`)
2. **Add tokens to TOKEN_REGISTRY** (same file)
3. **Deploy escrow contract** using `DeployCreate2.s.sol` to ensure deterministic address
4. **Set environment variables**:
   - Add to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS=...`
   - Set `ESCROW_CONTRACT_196=0x...`
   - Set `RPC_URL_196=...`
5. **Add chain to database** (manually or via admin API)
6. **Add to Wagmi config** (frontend) if user-facing
7. **Verify** via `/escrow/supported-chains` endpoint

---

### FILE PATHS - KEY LOCATIONS

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Chain registry | `/packages/shared/src/chains.ts` |
| Escrow config | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts` |
| Escrow service | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.service.ts` |
| Escrow client (viem) | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts` |

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me compile everything:

## Comprehensive Blockchain Chain Integration Analysis - ClawQuest

Based on my thorough exploration of the ClawQuest monorepo, here's a detailed analysis of how blockchain chains are currently integrated:

---

### 1. SUPPORTED CHAINS CONFIGURATION

**Primary Chain Registry**: `/Users/vuongluu/Documents/jobs/lab3/ceo/clawquest/packages/shared/src/chains.ts`

**Currently Supported Chains**:
- **Base** (Chain ID: 8453) - Mainnet
- **Base Sepolia** (Chain ID: 84532) - Testnet ✓ DEFAULT
- **BNB Smart Chain (BSC)** (Chain ID: 56) - Mainnet
- **BSC Testnet** (Chain ID: 97) - Testnet
- **Ethereum** (Chain ID: 1) - Mainnet (reference only)
- **Arbitrum** (Chain ID: 42161) - Mainnet (reference only)
- **Polygon** (Chain ID: 137) - Mainnet (reference only)

**Escrow-Enabled Chains** (actively used):
- `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS = [8453, 84532, 56, 97]` (Base + BSC)

**XLayer Support**: Currently only mapped in OKX API integration (onchain.service.ts) for wallet portfolio queries with chain ID 196, but NOT in escrow or core chain config.

---

### 2. TOKEN CONFIGURATION BY CHAIN

**Token Registry**: `/packages/shared/src/chains.ts` lines 88-130

**Token Addresses**:

| Chain | Token | Address | Decimals |
|-------|-------|---------|----------|
| Base (8453) | USDC | 0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913 | 6 |
| Base (8453) | USDT | 0xfde4C96c8593536E31F229EA8f37b2ADa2699bb2 | 18 |
| Base (8453) | ETH (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| Base Sepolia (84532) | USDC | 0xAA87A3f8E6017F1f02af0f306B765FCfBeCac3E4 | 6 |
| Base Sepolia (84532) | ETH (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| BNB (56) | USDC | 0x8AC76a51cc950d9822D68b83fE1Ad97B32Cd580d | 18 |
| BNB (56) | USDT | 0x55d398326f99059fF775485246999027B3197955 | 18 |
| BNB (56) | BNB (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| BSC Testnet (97) | USDT | 0x337610d27c682E347C9cD60BD4b3b107C9d34dDd | 18 |
| BSC Testnet (97) | tBNB (native) | 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 | 18 |
| Polygon (137) | USDC | 0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359 | 6 |
| Polygon (137) | USDT | 0xc2132D05D31c914a87C6611C10748AEb04B58e8F | 6 |

**Token Helper Functions**:
- `getTokenInfo(chainId, symbol)` - Get token by chain + symbol
- `getTokenByAddress(chainId, address)` - Get token by chain + contract address
- `isNativeToken(address)` - Check if address is native token (0x0000...)

---

### 3. SMART CONTRACT DEPLOYMENT

**Escrow Contract**: `/Users/vuongluu/Documents/jobs/lab3/ceo/clawquest/contracts/src/ClawQuestEscrow.sol`

**Key Features**:
- UUPS Upgradeable proxy pattern
- Single instance per chain
- Role-based access control (OPERATOR_ROLE, PAUSER_ROLE, UPGRADER_ROLE)
- Token allowlist (configurable per chain)
- Multi-sponsor deposit model with sub-questIds

**Deploy Scripts**:
1. `/contracts/script/Deploy.s.sol` - Standard deployment
2. `/contracts/script/DeployCreate2.s.sol` - Deterministic CREATE2 (same address across chains)

**Foundry Configuration**: `/contracts/foundry.toml`
```toml
[rpc_endpoints]
base_sepolia = "https://sepolia.base.org"
base = "https://mainnet.base.org"
bnb = "https://bsc-dataseed.binance.org"
bsc_testnet = "https://data-seed-prebsc-1-s1.binance.org:8545"

[etherscan]
base_sepolia = { key = "${BASESCAN_API_KEY}" }
base = { key = "${BASESCAN_API_KEY}" }
bnb = { key = "${BSCSCAN_API_KEY}" }
bsc_testnet = { key = "${BSCSCAN_API_KEY}" }
```

**Contract Deployment Configuration**:
```env
# Base Sepolia (testnet) — LIVE contract
REDACTED

# Per-chain contract addresses (optional):
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_8453=0x...    (Base mainnet)
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_97=0x...      (BSC Testnet)

# Shared CREATE2 address (fallback for all chains):
# ESCROW_CONTRACT=0x...
```

---

### 4. ESCROW/FUNDING SYSTEM

**Escrow Configuration Module**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts`

**Active Chains Determination**:
1. `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` env var (comma-separated) → validated against SUPPORTED_CHAINS
2. Fallback: `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` constant filtered by `ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE` (testnet|mainnet)
3. Default chain: `ESCROW_CHAIN_ID` env var (falls back to first active chain)

**RPC URL Resolution Priority**:
1. `RPC_URL_<chainId>` env vars (canonical format)
2. Legacy named env vars: `RPC_URL_BASE`, `RPC_URL_BASE_SEPOLIA`, `RPC_URL_BNB`, `RPC_URL_BSC_TESTNET`
3. Fallback: Public RPC from `SUPPORTED_CHAINS`

**RPC Manager Service**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/rpc-manager.service.ts`
- Loads active RPCs from database (`ChainRpc` table)
- Health tracking with 5-minute cooldown after 3 failures
- Fallback to escrow.config if DB RPCs unavailable
- Multiple RPCs per chain supported with priority-based selection

**Escrow Client**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts`
- Uses viem for Ethereum interactions
- Creates public and wallet clients per chain
- Supports fallback transports across multiple RPCs
- Chain mapping for known chains (Base, BSC, Ethereum, etc.)
- Dynamic chain definition from SUPPORTED_CHAINS for unknown chains

**Escrow Routes**: `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.routes.ts`
- `GET /escrow/supported-chains` - Returns active chains for environment
- `GET /escrow/deposit-params/:questId` - Frontend parameters for deposit transactions
- Distribution and refund operations for operators

---

### 5. DATABASE SCHEMA

**Chain-Related Models**: `/apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`

```prisma
model Chain {
  id              Int              @id
  name            String
  shortName       String
  nativeCurrency  Json
  explorerUrl     String
  isTestnet       Boolean          @default(false)
  isEscrowEnabled Boolean          @default(false)
  isActive        Boolean          @default(true)
  createdAt       DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime         @updatedAt
  rpcs            ChainRpc[]
  contracts       EscrowContract[]
}

model ChainRpc {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  chainId   Int
  url       String
  provider  String?
  priority  Int      @default(10)
  isActive  Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
  chain     Chain    @relation(fields: [chainId])
  @@unique([chainId, url])
  @@index([chainId, isActive, priority])
}

model EscrowContract {
  id         String    @id @default(uuid())
  chainId    Int
  address    String
  isActive   Boolean   @default(true)
  deployedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime  @updatedAt
  chain      Chain     @relation(fields: [chainId])
  @@unique([chainId, address])
  @@index([chainId, isActive])
}

model EscrowCursor {
  chainId   Int      @id
  lastBlock BigInt
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}

model Quest {
  // ... other fields ...
  cryptoChainId        Int?
  cryptoTxHash         String?
  fundedAmount         Int?
  fundedAt             DateTime?
  fundingMethod        String?       // "stripe" or "crypto"
  fundingStatus        String        // "unfunded", "pending", "partial", "confirmed"
  tokenAddress         String?
  tokenDecimals        Int?
  tokenProvider        String?       // for USDT vs USDC selection
  network              String?       // e.g. "Base", "BNB Smart Chain"
  totalFunded          Decimal       @db.Decimal(36, 18)
  refundAmount         Int?
  refundStatus         String?
  refundTxHash         String?
  refundedAt           DateTime?
  escrowQuestId        String?       // quest ID for on-chain escrow
}

model QuestDeposit {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  questId       String
  escrowQuestId String
  userId        String
  amount        Decimal  @db.Decimal(36, 18)
  tokenAddress  String
  chainId       Int
  txHash        String?
  walletAddress String   @default("")
  status        String   // "pending", "confirmed", "refunded"
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  quest         Quest    @relation(fields: [questId])
  user          User     @relation(fields: [userId])
}

model WalletLink {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  address   String
  chainId   Int?      // optional: link to specific chain
  isPrimary Boolean   @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
  user      User      @relation(fields: [userId])
  @@unique([userId, address])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([address])
}
```

**Key Migration**: `/apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260305085350_chain_rpc/migration.sql`
- Adds Chain, ChainRpc, and EscrowContract tables
- Enables multi-RPC fallback per chain
- Supports multiple contract addresses per chain

---

### 6. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

**Core Chain Configuration** (`.env.example`):
```env
# Network mode (testnet = Base Sepolia + BSC Testnet, mainnet = Base + BNB)
ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE=testnet                # testnet | mainnet
VITE_ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE=testnet           # frontend counterpart

# Active chains (comma-separated chain IDs, source of truth)
ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS=84532                  # add chains: 84532,8453,56
ESCROW_CHAIN_ID=84532                   # default chain (84532=Base Sepolia, 8453=Base)

# Contract addresses
REDACTED
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_97=0x...      (BSC Testnet)
# ESCROW_CONTRACT_8453=0x...    (Base mainnet)
ESCROW_CONTRACT=""                      # Shared CREATE2 fallback

# Operator key
# OPERATOR_PRIVATE_KEY="0x..."

# Escrow poller
ESCROW_POLL_INTERVAL=15000              # ms between polls (default 15s)
ESCROW_CONFIRMATION_BLOCKS=5            # blocks behind tip (default 5)

# Custom RPC URLs (optional — defaults to public endpoints)
# RPC_URL_8453=https://...              # canonical format
# RPC_URL_84532=https://...
# RPC_URL_BNB=https://...               # legacy format
# RPC_URL_BSC_TESTNET=https://...
```

---

### 7. FRONTEND INTEGRATION

**Wagmi Configuration**: `/apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`
- Uses RainbowKit with viem
- Chains determined by `VITE_ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE`
- Testnet: `[baseSepolia, bscTestnet]`
- Mainnet: `[base, bsc]`
- Requires `VITE_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID`

**Escrow Routes**: `/apps/dashboard/src/routes/_authenticated/quests/$questId/fund.tsx`
- Handles deposit flow with chain/token selection
- Creates pending QuestDeposit records
- Integrates with wagmi for wallet connection

---

### 8. API ENDPOINTS

**Escrow Routes** (`/escrow` prefix):
- `GET /supported-chains` - List active chains and network mode
- `GET /deposit-params/:questId` - Params for deposit tx (questId, contract, token, chain)
- `POST /distribute/:questId` - Operator-only: distribute rewards
- `POST /refund/:questId` - Operator-only: refund deposits
- `GET /status/:questId` - Read on-chain escrow status

**Onchain Routes** (`/onchain` prefix):
- Portfolio queries via OKX API
- Supports chains: Ethereum, BSC, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, XLayer (196), Solana, Optimism, Avalanche

---

### 9. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Multi-Chain Design**:
1. **Shared Constants** (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`) - Single source of truth for chain metadata
2. **Per-Chain Contracts** - Different escrow contract per chain (CREATE2 for determinism)
3. **Per-Chain RPCs** - Database-backed RPC management with health tracking
4. **Sub-QuestIds** - Multi-sponsor deposits use keccak256(questId + sponsorId)

**Amount Handling**:
- Human-readable amounts in API/DB (e.g., 500 USDC)
- Convert to smallest unit on-chain using token decimals
- Helper functions: `toSmallestUnit()`, `fromSmallestUnit()`

**UUID → bytes32 Conversion**:
- Databases use UUIDs, smart contracts use bytes32
- `/packages/shared/src/escrow-utils.ts` handles conversion
- Quest ID: left-pad to 64 hex chars
- Sub-quest ID: keccak256(questIdBytes32 + sponsorIdBytes32)

---

### 10. MISSING/INCOMPLETE FEATURES

1. **XLayer (Chain ID 196)**: Currently only in portfolio mapping, not in escrow.config or token registry
2. **Multiple Tokens per Chain**: Token selection logic exists but not fully exposed in UI
3. **Mainnet Deployment**: Base (8453) and BNB (56) contracts not yet configured in .env
4. **Chain Management API**: No admin endpoints to dynamically add/remove chains at runtime
5. **Polygon/Arbitrum/Ethereum**: Defined in SUPPORTED_CHAINS but no deployed contracts

---

### 11. DEPLOYMENT CHECKLIST FOR NEW CHAINS

To add a new chain (e.g., XLayer 196):

1. **Add to SUPPORTED_CHAINS** (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`)
2. **Add tokens to TOKEN_REGISTRY** (same file)
3. **Deploy escrow contract** using `DeployCreate2.s.sol` to ensure deterministic address
4. **Set environment variables**:
   - Add to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS=...`
   - Set `ESCROW_CONTRACT_196=0x...`
   - Set `RPC_URL_196=...`
5. **Add chain to database** (manually or via admin API)
6. **Add to Wagmi config** (frontend) if user-facing
7. **Verify** via `/escrow/supported-chains` endpoint

---

### FILE PATHS - KEY LOCATIONS

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Chain registry | `/packages/shared/src/chains.ts` |
| Escrow config | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts` |
| Escrow service | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.service.ts` |
| Escrow client (viem) | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts` |
| RPC manager | `/apps/api/src/modules/escrow/rpc-manager.service.ts` |
| Escrow contract | `/contracts/src/ClawQuestEscrow.sol` |
| Deploy scripts | `/contracts/script/Deploy.s.sol`, `DeployCreate2.s.sol` |
| Foundry config | `/contracts/foundry.toml` |
| Prisma schema | `/apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` |
| Chain migration | `/apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260305085350_chain_rpc/` |
| Wagmi frontend | `/apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts` |
| Environment template | `/.env.example` |

Tôi đã khám phá xong codebase. Đây là tóm tắt hiện trạng:

**Chains đang support**: Base (8453), Base Sepolia (84532), BSC (56), BSC Testnet (97)

- **B) Cả Mainnet (196) + Testnet (1952)** — giống BSC có cả mainnet + testnet để dev/test trước
- **C) Testnet trước (1952), mainnet sau** — deploy testnet để validate flow, rồi thêm mainnet sau

1→# Onchain Settlement Layer
     2→
     3→All USDC rewards on ClawQuest settle onchain. This ensures trustless, verifiable payouts between brands and agents.
     4→
     5→## Why Onchain?
     6→
     7→| Benefit | Explanation |
     8→| --- | --- |
     9→| **Trust** | Agents verify the transaction — no need to trust ClawQuest or the brand |
    10→| **Transparency** | Brands prove they paid. No "check is in the mail." |
    11→| **Auditability** | Anyone can audit the full payout history on a block explorer |
    12→| **Composability** | Other platforms can build on ClawQuest's payout data |
    13→
    14→## Payout Flow
    15→
    16→```
    17→1. Agent completes quest and submits proof
    18→2. Sponsor (or system) approves proof
    19→3. ClawQuest creates a PayoutRecord (status: pending)
    20→4. Smart contract executes transfer (USDC → agent owner wallet)
    21→5. Tx confirmed onchain → PayoutRecord updated (status: confirmed, txHash filled)
    22→6. Agent and brand can verify on block explorer
    23→```
    24→
    25→## Supported Chains
    26→
    27→| Chain | Chain ID | Token | Status |
    28→| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    29→| Base | 8453 | USDC | Supported |
    30→| Polygon | 137 | USDC | Planned |
    31→| Ethereum | 1 | USDC | Planned |
    32→| Arbitrum | 42161 | USDC | Planned |
    33→
    34→## Payout Record Data Model
    35→
    36→| Field | Type | Description |
    37→| --- | --- | --- |
    38→| `id` | UUID | Payout record ID |
    39→| `participationId` | UUID | Link to quest participation |
    40→| `chainId` | integer | Chain ID (e.g., 8453 for Base) |
    41→| `contractAddress` | string | USDC contract address |
    42→| `txHash` | string | Transaction hash (null if pending) |
    43→| `fromWallet` | string | Sponsor wallet address |
    44→| `toWallet` | string | Agent owner wallet address |
    45→| `amount` | string | Amount in smallest unit (wei) |
    46→| `tokenSymbol` | string | `"USDC"` |
    47→| `status` | string | `pending`, `submitted`, `confirmed`, `failed` |
    48→| `confirmedAt` | datetime | When the tx was confirmed onchain |
    49→
    50→## Payout Status Flow
    51→
    52→```
    53→pending → submitted → confirmed
    54→                   └→ failed
    55→```
    56→
    57→| Status | Description |
    58→| --- | --- |
    59→| `pending` | Record created, awaiting contract execution |
    60→| `submitted` | Transaction submitted to the chain |
    61→| `confirmed` | Transaction confirmed onchain |
    62→| `failed` | Transaction failed — will retry or escalate |
    63→
    64→## Verification
    65→
    66→### Query Payout History
    67→
    68→```http
    69→GET /payouts?questId={id}&agentId={id}&status={status}
    70→```
    71→
    72→Returns payout records with tx hashes, amounts, and chain info.
    73→
    74→### Verify a Specific Payout
    75→
    76→```http
    77→GET /payouts/{id}/verify
    78→```
    79→
    80→Returns the payout record with onchain verification status and a direct link to the block explorer:

content.thinking:
The user is asking about the deployment mechanism. They noticed that BSC and Base have the same contract address `0xF86f5498165D62E030540D6c5675b99` and want to know if it's CREATE2 or regular deploy with nonce matching.

Let me check the deploy scripts to understand which mechanism is being used.

content.text: Câu hỏi hay. Để tôi check deploy scripts hiện tại.

Đúng rồi — address `0xF86f...5b99` giống nhau trên cả BSC và Base vì project **đang dùng CREATE2** (`DeployCreate2.s.sol`). Cơ chế:

- **CREATE2**: `address = keccak256(0xff + deployer + salt + bytecodeHash)` — chỉ phụ thuộc vào deployer address, salt cố định, và bytecode giống nhau → ra cùng address trên mọi EVM chain
- **Deploy thường (CREATE)**: `address = keccak256(rlp(deployer, nonce))` — phụ thuộc vào nonce. Muốn cùng address thì phải đảm bảo deployer wallet có cùng nonce trên chain mới, rủi ro cao nếu đã dùng wallet đó trên chain khác

**Kết luận**: Nên dùng **CREATE2** (`DeployCreate2.s.sol`) cho X Layer để đảm bảo cùng address `0xF86f...5b99`. Chỉ cần:
- Cùng `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY`
- Cùng `ADMIN_ADDRESS` + `OPERATOR_ADDRESS`
- Cùng solc version + optimizer settings (đã pin trong foundry.toml)

Vậy tôi đủ context rồi. Để tôi đề xuất approaches.

content.text:
## Approaches

Tôi recommend **Approach A** vì phù hợp nhất với yêu cầu:

### Approach A: Full Integration (giống BSC) — Recommended

Thêm X Layer vào tất cả layers: shared chain registry → API escrow config → frontend wagmi → deploy guide.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Consistent với BSC pattern | Nhiều files thay đổi (~8 files) |
| User có thể fund quest qua X Layer ngay | Cần deploy contract trước khi go-live |
| CREATE2 đảm bảo cùng address | |

**Scope**: `chains.ts` + `escrow.config.ts` + `escrow.client.ts` + `wagmi.ts` + `foundry.toml` + `.env.example` + deploy guide doc

### Approach B: Backend-only + Deploy guide trước

Chỉ thêm chain registry + API config + deploy guide. Frontend thêm sau khi contract đã deploy thành công.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Ít risk hơn, incremental | Cần 2 PR |
| Không cần contract address ngay | User chưa fund qua X Layer được |

### Approach C: Config-only + Feature flag

Thêm tất cả code nhưng X Layer disabled by default, enable qua env var `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS`.

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Code sẵn, bật khi muốn | Over-engineering cho 1 chain |
| Zero risk cho production | Phức tạp hơn cần thiết |

---

**Recommendation**: **Approach A** — project đã có pattern rõ ràng từ BSC, chỉ cần follow y hệt. Env var `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` đã là feature flag tự nhiên rồi (không thêm 196 vào = không active). Bạn đồng ý approach A không?
Approach A, tuy nhiên tôi muốn deploy thử lên Testnet để kiểm tra address trước.

Tốt, thêm X Layer Testnet (1952) để verify address trước mainnet. Giờ tôi trình bày design từng phần.

## Design Section 1: Shared Chain Registry

Thêm X Layer Mainnet (196) + Testnet (1952) vào [packages/shared/src/chains.ts](packages/shared/src/chains.ts):

**Chains:**
```
X Layer Mainnet: chainId=196, name="X Layer", nativeCurrency=OKB, explorer=okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer
X Layer Testnet: chainId=1952, name="X Layer Testnet", nativeCurrency=OKB, explorer=okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer-test
```

**Tokens (chỉ 2 token, giống BSC):**
```
196 (Mainnet): USDC=0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22, USDT=0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d
1952 (Testnet): tokens TBD sau khi verify trên testnet
```

**ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS** thêm `196` (mainnet) và `1952` (testnet) vào danh sách hợp lệ.

Section 1 ổn không?
Ổn nhé

## Design Section 2: API Escrow Config

**Files thay đổi:**

1. **`escrow.config.ts`** — thêm legacy RPC mapping cho X Layer:
   - `RPC_URL_XLAYER` → chain 196
   - `RPC_URL_XLAYER_TESTNET` → chain 1952

2. **`escrow.client.ts`** — thêm X Layer vào viem chain mapping (hiện chỉ có Base, BSC, Ethereum). X Layer là OP Stack chain nên define custom chain với chainId=196, rpcUrl, explorer.

3. **`foundry.toml`** — thêm RPC endpoints + etherscan config cho X Layer (theo guide đã có).

4. **`.env.example`** — thêm:
   ```
   ESCROW_CONTRACT_196=0x...
   ESCROW_CONTRACT_1952=0x...
   RPC_URL_196=https://rpc.xlayer.tech
   RPC_URL_1952=https://testrpc.xlayer.tech
   ```

**Không thay đổi**: escrow.service.ts, escrow.routes.ts — đã generic, tự pick up chain mới từ config.

Section 2 ổn không?
<ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /Users/vuongluu/Documents/jobs/lab3/ceo/clawquest/.claude/settings.json in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>
ổn nhé

## Design Section 3: Frontend (Wagmi + Dashboard)

**`apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`** — thêm X Layer chain:
- viem chưa có built-in `xlayer` chain → define custom chain object (chainId, name, rpcUrls, blockExplorers, nativeCurrency=OKB)
- Mainnet mode: `[base, bsc, xlayer]`
- Testnet mode: `[baseSepolia, bscTestnet, xlayerTestnet]`

**Không cần thay đổi gì khác ở frontend** — fund flow, chain selector, token selector đã generic, tự hiển thị chain mới từ wagmi config + `/escrow/supported-chains` API.

Section 3 ổn không?
Ổn

## Design Section 4: Contract Deployment Guide

Đây là phần chỉ viết guide (không code), lưu vào `docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md` (update doc hiện có):

**Nội dung guide:**

1. **Prerequisites** — Foundry installed, OKB cho gas (cả testnet & mainnet)
2. **Env setup** — `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ADMIN_ADDRESS`, `OPERATOR_ADDRESS`, `USDC_ADDRESS`, `USDT_ADDRESS` cho X Layer
3. **Step 1: Testnet deploy** — `DeployCreate2.s.sol` → `--rpc-url https://testrpc.xlayer.tech` → verify address khớp `0xF86f...5b99`
4. **Step 2: Verify trên explorer** — check tx hash trên testnet explorer
5. **Step 3: Mainnet deploy** — cùng script, `--rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech --chain-id 196`
6. **Step 4: Update env vars** — set `ESCROW_CONTRACT_196`, `ESCROW_CONTRACT_1952`, thêm vào `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS`
7. **Troubleshooting** — OKB gas, RPC timeout, address mismatch

**Khác với guide cũ**: Dùng `DeployCreate2.s.sol` thay vì `Deploy.s.sol`, thêm step verify address deterministic.

Section 4 — đây là section cuối. Toàn bộ design ổn không?
Ổn nhé.

input.content:
# X Layer Chain Integration Design

## Summary

Add X Layer (Chain ID 196) mainnet + testnet (1952) support to ClawQuest, following the existing BSC integration pattern. Only USDC and USDT tokens. Contract deployment via CREATE2 for deterministic address matching existing chains. Testnet deploy first to verify address.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Shared chain registry (chains.ts) — add X Layer mainnet + testnet
- API escrow config — RPC mapping, viem chain definition
- Frontend wagmi — custom X Layer chain, chain selector
- Foundry config — RPC + etherscan endpoints
- Deploy guide — CREATE2 deployment steps for testnet → mainnet
- Environment variables — contract addresses, RPC URLs

**Out of scope:**
- Actual contract deployment (guide only)
- Admin dashboard changes (already generic)
- New API endpoints (existing ones are chain-agnostic)

## Design

### 1. Shared Chain Registry (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`)

Add to `SUPPORTED_CHAINS`:

```typescript
// X Layer Mainnet
{
  chainId: 196,
  name: 'X Layer',
  shortName: 'xlayer',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrl: 'https://rpc.xlayer.tech',
  explorerUrl: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer',
  isTestnet: false,
}
// X Layer Testnet
{
  chainId: 1952,
  name: 'X Layer Testnet',
  shortName: 'xlayer-testnet',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrl: 'https://testrpc.xlayer.tech',
  explorerUrl: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer-test',
  isTestnet: true,
}
```

Add to `TOKEN_REGISTRY`:

```typescript
// X Layer Mainnet (196)
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDC', address: '0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22', decimals: 6 }
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDT', address: '0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d', decimals: 18 }
// X Layer Testnet (1952) — tokens TBD after testnet verification
```

Add 196 and 1952 to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS`.

### 2. API Escrow Config

**`escrow.config.ts`** — add legacy RPC env var mapping:
- `RPC_URL_XLAYER` → chainId 196
- `RPC_URL_XLAYER_TESTNET` → chainId 1952

**`escrow.client.ts`** — add X Layer to viem chain mapping. Define custom chain (X Layer is OP Stack, not built-in to viem):

```typescript
const xlayer = defineChain({
  id: 196,
  name: 'X Layer',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrls: { default: { http: ['https://rpc.xlayer.tech'] } },
  blockExplorers: { default: { name: 'OKX Explorer', url: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer' } },
})
```

Same pattern for testnet (chainId 1952).

**No changes needed**: escrow.service.ts, escrow.routes.ts, rpc-manager.service.ts — already generic.

### 3. Frontend Wagmi (`apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`)

Define custom X Layer chains (same as escrow.client.ts pattern) and add to wagmi config:

- Mainnet mode: `[base, bsc, xlayer]`
- Testnet mode: `[baseSepolia, bscTestnet, xlayerTestnet]`

No other frontend changes needed — fund flow, chain selector, token selector are already generic.

### 4. Foundry Config (`contracts/foundry.toml`)

```toml
[rpc_endpoints]
xlayer = "https://rpc.xlayer.tech"
xlayer_testnet = "https://testrpc.xlayer.tech"

[etherscan]
xlayer = { key = "${OKXSCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://www.okx.com/api/v5/explorer/contract/verify-source-code" }
```

### 5. Environment Variables (`.env.example`)

```env
# X Layer
ESCROW_CONTRACT_196=                    # X Layer mainnet contract
ESCROW_CONTRACT_1952=                   # X Layer testnet contract
# RPC_URL_196=https://rpc.xlayer.tech   # canonical format (or use legacy below)
# RPC_URL_XLAYER=https://rpc.xlayer.tech
# RPC_URL_1952=https://testrpc.xlayer.tech
# OKXSCAN_API_KEY=                      # for contract verification
```

### 6. Deploy Guide (`docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md`)

Update existing guide to use `DeployCreate2.s.sol` instead of `Deploy.s.sol`. Steps:

1. **Prerequisites**: Foundry, OKB for gas (testnet faucet + mainnet via OKX Exchange/Bridge)
2. **Env setup**: Export `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ADMIN_ADDRESS`, `OPERATOR_ADDRESS`, `USDC_ADDRESS`, `USDT_ADDRESS`
3. **Testnet deploy**: `forge script script/DeployCreate2.s.sol --rpc-url https://testrpc.xlayer.tech --broadcast --chain-id 1952`
4. **Verify address**: Confirm predicted proxy matches `0xF86f5498165D62E044964740F30540D6c5675b99`
5. **Mainnet deploy**: Same script with `--rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech --chain-id 196`
6. **Post-deploy**: Set `ESCROW_CONTRACT_196`, add `196` to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` in Railway
7. **Troubleshooting**: OKB gas, RPC timeout, address mismatch scenarios

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/shared/src/chains.ts` | Add X Layer chains + tokens |
| `apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts` | Add legacy RPC mapping |
| `apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts` | Add viem chain definition |
| `apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts` | Add X Layer to wagmi chains |
| `contracts/foundry.toml` | Add RPC + etherscan endpoints |
| `.env.example` | Add X Layer env vars |
| `docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md` | Rewrite with CREATE2 flow |

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| CREATE2 address mismatch on X Layer | Deploy testnet first to verify; same deployer/salt/bytecode guarantees same address |
| X Layer RPC instability | Fallback RPC: `https://xlayerrpc.okx.com`; DB-backed RPC manager supports multiple RPCs |
| OKB gas token confusion | Clear docs that X Layer uses OKB (not ETH) for gas |
| USDC/USDT decimal mismatch | Verify on-chain decimals match TOKEN_REGISTRY before going live |

content.text:
**Approved with minor issues (none blocking).**

The spec is solid and correctly follows the established BSC integration pattern. The codebase is already well-architected for multi-chain expansion -- `escrow.client.ts` has a dynamic `defineChain()` fallback (lines 30-48), `escrow.config.ts` resolves RPCs by chain ID automatically, and both API and frontend are chain-agnostic.

Three minor items for the implementer:

**1. Verify USDT decimals on-chain before merging (noted in spec risks, but critical to actually do)**

The spec claims USDT on X Layer = 18 decimals. Most chains use 6. If wrong, all deposit/distribution amounts will be off by 10^12. Run:
```bash
cast call 0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d "decimals()(uint8)" --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech
```

**2. Testnet RPC URL inconsistency**

The spec uses `https://testrpc.xlayer.tech` everywhere, but the existing deploy guide at `docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md` line 10 uses `https://testrpc.xlayer.tech/terigon` (with `/terigon` path). One of these will fail. Pick the correct one and use it consistently.

**3. Missing NATIVE token entry in TOKEN_REGISTRY**

Every existing chain in `packages/shared/src/chains.ts` includes a `NATIVE` entry (OKB for X Layer). The spec only adds USDC and USDT. Add:
```typescript
NATIVE: { address: '0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000', decimals: 18, symbol: 'OKB', name: 'OKB' }
```

**Additional note:** The shared package test at `packages/shared/src/__tests__/chains.test.ts` lines 165-174 has explicit assertions on `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` contents. Adding 196 and 1952 to the constant will break those tests -- implementer needs to update them.

Everything else checks out: chain IDs are correct, file list is complete, the `escrow.client.ts` dynamic fallback handles unknown chains automatically, the scope is right-sized, and the deploy guide rewrite from `Deploy.s.sol` to `DeployCreate2.s.sol` is the correct call.

# X Layer Chain Integration Design

## Summary

Add X Layer (Chain ID 196) mainnet + testnet (1952) support to ClawQuest, following the existing BSC integration pattern. Only USDC and USDT tokens. Contract deployment via CREATE2 for deterministic address matching existing chains. Testnet deploy first to verify address.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Shared chain registry (chains.ts) — add X Layer mainnet + testnet
- API escrow config — RPC mapping, viem chain definition
- Frontend wagmi — custom X Layer chain, chain selector
- Foundry config — RPC + etherscan endpoints
- Deploy guide — CREATE2 deployment steps for testnet → mainnet
- Environment variables — contract addresses, RPC URLs

**Out of scope:**
- Actual contract deployment (guide only)
- Admin dashboard changes (already generic)
- New API endpoints (existing ones are chain-agnostic)

## Design

### 1. Shared Chain Registry (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`)

Add to `SUPPORTED_CHAINS`:

```typescript
// X Layer Mainnet
{
  chainId: 196,
  name: 'X Layer',
  shortName: 'xlayer',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrl: 'https://rpc.xlayer.tech',
  explorerUrl: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer',
  isTestnet: false,
}
// X Layer Testnet
{
  chainId: 1952,
  name: 'X Layer Testnet',
  shortName: 'xlayer-testnet',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrl: 'https://testrpc.xlayer.tech',
  explorerUrl: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer-test',
  isTestnet: true,
}
```

Add to `TOKEN_REGISTRY`:

```typescript
// X Layer Mainnet (196)
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDC', address: '0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22', decimals: 6 }
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDT', address: '0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d', decimals: 18 }
// X Layer Testnet (1952) — tokens TBD after testnet verification
```

Add 196 and 1952 to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS`.

### 2. API Escrow Config

**`escrow.config.ts`** — add legacy RPC env var mapping:
- `RPC_URL_XLAYER` → chainId 196
- `RPC_URL_XLAYER_TESTNET` → chainId 1952

**`escrow.client.ts`** — add X Layer to viem chain mapping. Define custom chain (X Layer is OP Stack, not built-in to viem):

```typescript
const xlayer = defineChain({
  id: 196,
  name: 'X Layer',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrls: { default: { http: ['https://rpc.xlayer.tech'] } },
  blockExplorers: { default: { name: 'OKX Explorer', url: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer' } },
})
```

Same pattern for testnet (chainId 1952).

**No changes needed**: escrow.service.ts, escrow.routes.ts, rpc-manager.service.ts — already generic.

### 3. Frontend Wagmi (`apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`)

Define custom X Layer chains (same as escrow.client.ts pattern) and add to wagmi config:

- Mainnet mode: `[base, bsc, xlayer]`
- Testnet mode: `[baseSepolia, bscTestnet, xlayerTestnet]`

No other frontend changes needed — fund flow, chain selector, token selector are already generic.

### 4. Foundry Config (`contracts/foundry.toml`)

```toml
[rpc_endpoints]
xlayer = "https://rpc.xlayer.tech"
xlayer_testnet = "https://testrpc.xlayer.tech"

[etherscan]
xlayer = { key = "${OKXSCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://www.okx.com/api/v5/explorer/contract/verify-source-code" }
```

### 5. Environment Variables (`.env.example`)

```env
# X Layer
ESCROW_CONTRACT_196=                    # X Layer mainnet contract
ESCROW_CONTRACT_1952=                   # X Layer testnet contract
# RPC_URL_196=https://rpc.xlayer.tech   # canonical format (or use legacy below)
# RPC_URL_XLAYER=https://rpc.xlayer.tech
# RPC_URL_1952=https://testrpc.xlayer.tech
# OKXSCAN_API_KEY=                      # for contract verification
```

### 6. Deploy Guide (`docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md`)

Update existing guide to use `DeployCreate2.s.sol` instead of `Deploy.s.sol`. Steps:

1. **Prerequisites**: Foundry, OKB for gas (testnet faucet + mainnet via OKX Exchange/Bridge)
2. **Env setup**: Export `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ADMIN_ADDRESS`, `OPERATOR_ADDRESS`, `USDC_ADDRESS`, `USDT_ADDRESS`
3. **Testnet deploy**: `forge script script/DeployCreate2.s.sol --rpc-url https://testrpc.xlayer.tech --broadcast --chain-id 1952`
4. **Verify address**: Confirm predicted proxy matches `0xF86f5498165D62E044964740F30540D6c5675b99`
5. **Mainnet deploy**: Same script with `--rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech --chain-id 196`
6. **Post-deploy**: Set `ESCROW_CONTRACT_196`, add `196` to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` in Railway
7. **Troubleshooting**: OKB gas, RPC timeout, address mismatch scenarios

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/shared/src/chains.ts` | Add X Layer chains + tokens |
| `apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts` | Add legacy RPC mapping |
| `apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts` | Add viem chain definition |
| `apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts` | Add X Layer to wagmi chains |
| `contracts/foundry.toml` | Add RPC + etherscan endpoints |
| `.env.example` | Add X Layer env vars |
| `docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md` | Rewrite with CREATE2 flow |

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| CREATE2 address mismatch on X Layer | Deploy testnet first to verify; same deployer/salt/bytecode guarantees same address |
| X Layer RPC instability | Fallback RPC: `https://xlayerrpc.okx.com`; DB-backed RPC manager supports multiple RPCs |
| OKB gas token confusion | Clear docs that X Layer uses OKB (not ETH) for gas |
| USDC/USDT decimal mismatch | Verify on-chain decimals match TOKEN_REGISTRY before going live |

lines: +{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDT', address: '0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d', decimals: 18 }  // verify on-chain!
// X Layer Testnet (1952) — tokens TBD after testnet verification
+> **Important**: Verify USDT decimals on-chain before merging:
+> Most chains use 6 decimals for USDT. If wrong, deposit/distribution amounts will be off by 10^12.

| `docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md` | Rewrite with CREATE2 flow |
| `packages/shared/src/__tests__/chains.test.ts` | Update ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS assertions |

**In scope:**
- Shared chain registry (chains.ts) — add X Layer mainnet + testnet
- API escrow config — RPC mapping, viem chain definition
- Frontend wagmi — custom X Layer chain, chain selector
- Foundry config — RPC + etherscan endpoints
- Deploy guide — CREATE2 deployment steps for testnet → mainnet
- Environment variables — contract addresses, RPC URLs

**Out of scope:**
- Actual contract deployment (guide only)
- Admin dashboard changes (already generic)
- New API endpoints (existing ones are chain-agnostic)

## Design

### 1. Shared Chain Registry (`packages/shared/src/chains.ts`)

Add to `SUPPORTED_CHAINS`:

```typescript
// X Layer Mainnet
{
  chainId: 196,
  name: 'X Layer',
  shortName: 'xlayer',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrl: 'https://rpc.xlayer.tech',
  explorerUrl: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer',
  isTestnet: false,
}
// X Layer Testnet
{
  chainId: 1952,
  name: 'X Layer Testnet',
  shortName: 'xlayer-testnet',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrl: 'https://testrpc.xlayer.tech',
  explorerUrl: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer-test',
  isTestnet: true,
}
```

Add to `TOKEN_REGISTRY`:

```typescript
// X Layer Mainnet (196)
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'OKB', address: '0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000', decimals: 18 }  // native
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDC', address: '0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22', decimals: 6 }
{ chainId: 196, symbol: 'USDT', address: '0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d', decimals: 18 }  // verify on-chain!
// X Layer Testnet (1952) — tokens TBD after testnet verification
```

> **Important**: Verify USDT decimals on-chain before merging:
> `cast call 0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d "decimals()(uint8)" --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech`
> Most chains use 6 decimals for USDT. If wrong, deposit/distribution amounts will be off by 10^12.

Add 196 and 1952 to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS`.

### 2. API Escrow Config

**`escrow.config.ts`** — add legacy RPC env var mapping:
- `RPC_URL_XLAYER` → chainId 196
- `RPC_URL_XLAYER_TESTNET` → chainId 1952

**`escrow.client.ts`** — add X Layer to viem chain mapping. Define custom chain (X Layer is OP Stack, not built-in to viem):

```typescript
const xlayer = defineChain({
  id: 196,
  name: 'X Layer',
  nativeCurrency: { name: 'OKB', symbol: 'OKB', decimals: 18 },
  rpcUrls: { default: { http: ['https://rpc.xlayer.tech'] } },
  blockExplorers: { default: { name: 'OKX Explorer', url: 'https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer' } },
})
```

Same pattern for testnet (chainId 1952).

**No changes needed**: escrow.service.ts, escrow.routes.ts, rpc-manager.service.ts — already generic.

### 3. Frontend Wagmi (`apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`)

Define custom X Layer chains (same as escrow.client.ts pattern) and add to wagmi config:

- Mainnet mode: `[base, bsc, xlayer]`
- Testnet mode: `[baseSepolia, bscTestnet, xlayerTestnet]`

No other frontend changes needed — fund flow, chain selector, token selector are already generic.

### 4. Foundry Config (`contracts/foundry.toml`)

```toml
[rpc_endpoints]
xlayer = "https://rpc.xlayer.tech"
xlayer_testnet = "https://testrpc.xlayer.tech"

[etherscan]
xlayer = { key = "${OKXSCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://www.okx.com/api/v5/explorer/contract/verify-source-code" }
```

### 5. Environment Variables (`.env.example`)

```env
# X Layer
ESCROW_CONTRACT_196=                    # X Layer mainnet contract
ESCROW_CONTRACT_1952=                   # X Layer testnet contract
# RPC_URL_196=https://rpc.xlayer.tech   # canonical format (or use legacy below)
# RPC_URL_XLAYER=https://rpc.xlayer.tech
# RPC_URL_1952=https://testrpc.xlayer.tech
# OKXSCAN_API_KEY=                      # for contract verification
```

### 6. Deploy Guide (`docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md`)

Update existing guide to use `DeployCreate2.s.sol` instead of `Deploy.s.sol`. Steps:

1. **Prerequisites**: Foundry, OKB for gas (testnet faucet + mainnet via OKX Exchange/Bridge)
2. **Env setup**: Export `DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ADMIN_ADDRESS`, `OPERATOR_ADDRESS`, `USDC_ADDRESS`, `USDT_ADDRESS`
3. **Testnet deploy**: `forge script script/DeployCreate2.s.sol --rpc-url https://testrpc.xlayer.tech --broadcast --chain-id 1952`
4. **Verify address**: Confirm predicted proxy matches `0xF86f5498165D62E044964740F30540D6c5675b99`
5. **Mainnet deploy**: Same script with `--rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech --chain-id 196`
6. **Post-deploy**: Set `ESCROW_CONTRACT_196`, add `196` to `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` in Railway
7. **Troubleshooting**: OKB gas, RPC timeout, address mismatch scenarios

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/shared/src/chains.ts` | Add X Layer chains + tokens |
| `apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.config.ts` | Add legacy RPC mapping |
| `apps/api/src/modules/escrow/escrow.client.ts` | Add viem chain definition |
| `apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts` | Add X Layer to wagmi chains |
| `contracts/foundry.toml` | Add RPC + etherscan endpoints |
| `.env.example` | Add X Layer env vars |
| `docs/xlayer-deploy-guide.md` | Rewrite with CREATE2 flow |
| `packages/shared/src/__tests__/chains.test.ts` | Update ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS assertions |

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| CREATE2 address mismatch on X Layer | Deploy testnet first to verify; same deployer/salt/bytecode guarantees same address |
| X Layer RPC instability | Fallback RPC: `https://xlayerrpc.okx.com`; DB-backed RPC manager supports multiple RPCs |
| OKB gas token confusion | Clear docs that X Layer uses OKB (not ETH) for gas |
| USDC/USDT decimal mismatch | Verify on-chain decimals match TOKEN_REGISTRY before going live |

| OKB gas token confusion | Clear docs that X Layer uses OKB (not ETH) for gas |
| USDC/USDT decimal mismatch | Verify on-chain decimals match TOKEN_REGISTRY before going live |
+| Testnet RPC path | Verify correct URL: `testrpc.xlayer.tech` vs `testrpc.xlayer.tech/terigon` — test both |
+| Chains test breakage | Update `packages/shared/src/__tests__/chains.test.ts` ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS assertions |

1→# Chain Expansion Guide
     2→
     3→How to add a new EVM chain to ClawQuest escrow.
     4→
     5→## Prerequisites
     6→
     7→- Deployer private key (same key used for all chains)
     8→- Admin multisig address (same across chains)
     9→- Operator hot wallet address (same across chains)
    10→- USDC/USDT token addresses on the target chain
    11→- Gas funds for deployer + operator on the new chain
    12→
    13→## Step 1: Deploy Contract (CREATE2)
    14→
    15→Deploy using the deterministic CREATE2 script. Same deployer + same salt = same address.
    16→
    17→```bash
    18→cd contracts
    19→
    20→# Set env vars
    21→export DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY=0x...
    22→export ADMIN_ADDRESS=0x...
    23→export OPERATOR_ADDRESS=0x...
    24→export USDC_ADDRESS=0x...    # optional
    25→export USDT_ADDRESS=0x...    # optional
    26→
    27→# Deploy (replace RPC_URL with target chain)
    28→forge script script/DeployCreate2.s.sol \
    29→  --rpc-url $RPC_URL \
    30→  --broadcast \
    31→  --verify
    32→```
    33→
    34→The output will show the proxy address. It should match `ESCROW_CONTRACT` from previous deployments.
    35→
    36→## Step 2: Fund Operator Gas
    37→
    38→Send native gas tokens to the operator address on the new chain. The operator needs gas to call `distribute()` and `refund()`.
    39→
    40→## Step 3: Configure Token Allowlist
    41→
    42→If deployer != admin (production), the admin multisig must call:
    43→
    44→```solidity
    45→escrow.setTokenAllowed(USDC_ADDRESS, true);
    46→escrow.setTokenAllowed(USDT_ADDRESS, true);  // if applicable
    47→```
    48→
    49→## Step 4: Add Chain to API
    50→
    51→On Railway (or your deployment platform), update the env var:
    52→
    53→```
    54→# Before
    55→ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS=84532,8453
    56→
    57→# After (add new chain ID)
    58→ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS=84532,8453,42161
    59→```
    60→
    61→If the chain needs a custom RPC URL (recommended for production):
    62→
    63→```
    64→RPC_URL_42161=https://arb-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/YOUR_KEY
    65→```
    66→
    67→## Step 5: Restart API
    68→
    69→Restart the API service. On startup you should see:
    70→
    71→```
    72→[escrow:config] Active chains: [84532, 8453, 42161] (mode: mainnet)
    73→[escrow:poller] Starting for chains [84532, 8453, 42161] (interval: 15000ms, confirmations: 5)
    74→```
    75→
    76→## Step 6: Verify
    77→
    78→Check the health endpoint:
    79→
    80→```bash
    81→curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" https://api.clawquest.ai/escrow/health
    82→```
    83→
    84→Expected response includes the new chain in `activeChainIds` and `poller.chains`.
    85→
    86→Check supported chains endpoint:
    87→
    88→```bash
    89→curl https://api.clawquest.ai/escrow/supported-chains
    90→```
    91→
    92→## Adding Chain to Frontend
    93→
    94→The frontend chain list is hardcoded in `apps/dashboard/src/lib/wagmi.ts`. To add a new chain:
    95→
    96→1. Import the chain from `viem/chains`
    97→2. Add it to the appropriate array (testnet/mainnet)
    98→3. Add chain metadata to `packages/shared/src/chains.ts` if not already present
    99→
   100→## Environment Variables Reference
   101→
   102→| Variable | Required | Description |
   103→|----------|----------|-------------|
   104→| `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` | Yes | Comma-separated active chain IDs |
   105→| `ESCROW_CONTRACT` | Yes | CREATE2 proxy address (shared all chains) |
   106→| `ESCROW_CONTRACT_<chainId>` | No | Per-chain override (legacy) |
   107→| `ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE` | Yes | `testnet` or `mainnet` |
   108→| `OPERATOR_PRIVATE_KEY` | Yes | Operator hot wallet key |
   109→| `RPC_URL_<chainId>` | No | Custom RPC URL per chain |
   110→
   111→## Troubleshooting
   112→
   113→**Poller not picking up new chain**: Check that `ESCROW_CHAIN_IDS` includes the chain ID and `ESCROW_NETWORK_MODE` matches (testnet chains need `testnet` mode).
   114→
   115→**CREATE2 address mismatch**: Ensure you're using the exact same deployer key, solc version (0.8.24), and optimizer settings (200 runs) as previous deployments.
   116→
   117→**No contract address for chain**: Set `ESCROW_CONTRACT` (shared) or `ESCROW_CONTRACT_<chainId>` (per-chain override).
   118→

1→# Deploy ClawQuestEscrow on X Layer — Getting the TX Hash
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→X Layer = Ethereum L2 (OP Stack), **EVM-equivalent** — deploying Solidity contracts works exactly like Ethereum.
     6→
     7→| Info | Mainnet | Testnet |
     8→|------|---------|---------|
     9→| **Chain ID** | `196` (0xC4) | `1952` (0x7A0) |
    10→| **RPC** | `https://rpc.xlayer.tech` | `https://testrpc.xlayer.tech/terigon` |
    11→| **Explorer** | [okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer](https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer) | [okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer-test](https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer-test) |
    12→| **Gas Token** | **OKB** | OKB (testnet) |
    13→
    14→---
    15→
    16→## Prerequisites
    17→
    18→### 1. Foundry (already in the project)
    19→```bash
    20→# Check if installed
    21→forge --version
    22→
    23→# If not installed:
    24→curl -L https://foundry.paradigm.xyz | bash
    25→foundryup
    26→```
    27→
    28→### 2. OKB for Gas Fees
    29→> [!CAUTION]
    30→> X Layer uses **OKB** as its gas token (NOT ETH). You need OKB in your deployer wallet.
    31→
    32→**How to get OKB:**
    33→- **Buy on OKX Exchange** → withdraw to deployer wallet on X Layer mainnet
    34→- **Bridge from Ethereum** → use [X Layer Bridge](https://www.okx.com/web3/bridge) to bridge OKB from ETH L1 to X Layer L2
    35→- Amount needed: ~0.01-0.05 OKB for deployment (gas is very cheap)
    36→
    37→### 3. Deployer Private Key
    38→You need the private key of a wallet with OKB on X Layer mainnet.
    39→
    40→### 4. Token Addresses on X Layer Mainnet
    41→
    42→| Token | Address |
    43→|-------|---------|
    44→| **USDC** | `0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22` |
    45→| **USDC.e** | `0xA8CE8aee21bC2A48a5EF670afCc9274C7bbbC035` |
    46→| **USDT** | `0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d` |
    47→| **USDT0** | `0x779Ded0c9e1022225f8E0630b35a9b54bE713736` |
    48→| **WOKB** | `0xe538905cf8410324e03A5A23C1c177a474D59b2b` |
    49→| **WETH** | `0x5A77f1443D16ee5761d310e38b62f77f726bC71c` |
    50→| **WBTC** | `0xEA034fb02eB1808C2cc3adbC15f447B93CbE08e1` |
    51→| **DAI** | `0xC5015b9d9161Dca7e18e32f6f25C4aD850731Fd4` |
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→## Step-by-Step Deploy
    56→
    57→### Step 1: Add X Layer to `foundry.toml`
    58→
    59→Open `contracts/foundry.toml` and add:
    60→
    61→```diff
    62→ [rpc_endpoints]
    63→ base_sepolia = "https://sepolia.base.org"
    64→ base = "https://mainnet.base.org"
    65→ bnb = "https://bsc-dataseed.binance.org"
    66→ bsc_testnet = "https://data-seed-prebsc-1-s1.binance.org:8545"
    67→+xlayer = "https://rpc.xlayer.tech"
    68→+xlayer_testnet = "https://testrpc.xlayer.tech/terigon"
    69→
    70→ [etherscan]
    71→ base_sepolia = { key = "${BASESCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://api-sepolia.basescan.org/api" }
    72→ base = { key = "${BASESCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://api.basescan.org/api" }
    73→ bnb = { key = "${BSCSCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://api.bscscan.com/api" }
    74→ bsc_testnet = { key = "${BSCSCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://api-testnet.bscscan.com/api" }
    75→+xlayer = { key = "${OKXSCAN_API_KEY}", url = "https://www.okx.com/api/v5/explorer/contract/verify-source-code" }
    76→```
    77→
    78→### Step 2: Set Environment Variables
    79→
    80→```bash
    81→# Export before running deploy
    82→export DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY="0x..."          # Deployer wallet private key
    83→export ADMIN_ADDRESS="0x..."                  # Admin wallet (multisig or same as deployer)
    84→export OPERATOR_ADDRESS="0x..."               # Backend hot wallet
    85→export USDC_ADDRESS="0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22"   # USDC on X Layer
    86→export USDT_ADDRESS="0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d"   # USDT on X Layer
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Step 3: Verify RPC Connection
    90→
    91→```bash
    92→# Check connection to X Layer mainnet
    93→cast chain-id --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech
    94→# Expected result: 196
    95→
    96→# Check deployer balance (OKB)
    97→cast balance $ADMIN_ADDRESS --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech
    98→# Must be > 0 (OKB needed for gas)
    99→```
   100→
   101→### Step 4: Deploy ClawQuestEscrow
   102→
   103→```bash
   104→cd contracts
   105→
   106→# Deploy using existing Deploy.s.sol script
   107→forge script script/Deploy.s.sol \
   108→  --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech \
   109→  --broadcast \
   110→  --chain-id 196 \
   111→  -vvvv
   112→```
   113→
   114→**Expected output:**
   115→```
   116→Deploying ClawQuestEscrow...
   117→  Admin:     0x...
   118→  Operator:  0x...
   119→  USDC:      0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22
   120→  USDT:      0x1E4a5963aBFD975d8c9021ce480b42188849D41d
   121→  Implementation deployed at: 0x...
   122→  Proxy deployed at: 0x...
   123→  USDC added to allowlist
   124→  USDT added to allowlist
   125→
   126→=== Deployment Complete ===
   127→Proxy (use this address): 0x...
   128→Implementation:           0x...
   129→```
   130→
   131→> [!IMPORTANT]
   132→> **Save the Transaction Hash** from the output — this is the TX Hash needed for the hackathon submission form!
   133→
   134→### Step 5: Verify TX Hash on Explorer
   135→
   136→Open URL:
   137→```
   138→https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer/tx/0x<YOUR_TX_HASH>
   139→```
   140→
   141→Verify:
   142→- ✅ Status: Success
   143→- ✅ Chain: X Layer (196)
   144→- ✅ Contract address is correct
   145→
   146→### Step 6 (Optional): Create Additional TX — Interact with Contract
   147→
   148→If you want additional TX hashes, you can interact with the deployed contract:
   149→
   150→```bash
   151→# Check if token is allowlisted
   152→cast call <PROXY_ADDRESS> \
   153→  "isTokenAllowed(address)(bool)" \
   154→  "0x74b7F16337b8972027F6196A17a631aC6dE26d22" \
   155→  --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech
   156→# Result: true
   157→```
   158→
   159→---
   160→
   161→## Alternatives — Fastest Way to Get a TX Hash
   162→
   163→If you don't have time for a full contract deployment, you can use `forge create` directly:
   164→
   165→### Option A: Deploy Simple Contract (30 seconds)
   166→
   167→```bash
   168→# Deploy implementation only (no proxy) — fastest
   169→forge create src/ClawQuestEscrow.sol:ClawQuestEscrow \
   170→  --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech \
   171→  --private-key $DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY \
   172→  --chain-id 196
   173→```
   174→
   175→### Option B: Simple Transfer (10 seconds)
   176→
   177→```bash
   178→# Send 0 OKB to yourself — creates a simple TX
   179→cast send $ADMIN_ADDRESS \
   180→  --value 0 \
   181→  --rpc-url https://rpc.xlayer.tech \
   182→  --private-key $DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY
   183→```
   184→
   185→> [!WARNING]
   186→> Option B should only be used as a backup if you're running out of time. Judges value TX from contract deployment/interaction more than simple transfers.
   187→
   188→---
   189→
   190→## Troubleshooting
   191→
   192→| Issue | Solution |
   193→|-------|----------|
   194→| `insufficient funds` | Add more OKB to wallet (X Layer gas token) |
   195→| `chain id mismatch` | Ensure `--chain-id 196` for mainnet |
   196→| RPC timeout | Try backup RPC: `https://xlayerrpc.okx.com` |
   197→| `nonce too low` | Add `--nonce <correct_nonce>` (check with `cast nonce`) |
   198→
   199→---
   200→
   201→## References
   202→
   203→| Resource | URL |
   204→|----------|-----|
   205→| X Layer Docs | [web3.okx.com/xlayer/docs](https://web3.okx.com/xlayer/docs/developer/build-on-xlayer/about-xlayer) |
   206→| Deploy with Foundry | [X Layer Foundry Guide](https://web3.okx.com/xlayer/docs/developer/deploy-a-smart-contract/deploy-with-foundry) |
   207→| X Layer Explorer | [okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer](https://www.okx.com/web3/explorer/xlayer) |
   208→| Token Addresses | [X Layer Contracts](https://web3.okx.com/xlayer/docs/developer/build-on-xlayer/contracts) |
   209→| Onchain OS Docs | [web3.okx.com/onchainos/dev-docs](https://web3.okx.com/onchainos/dev-docs/home/what-is-onchainos) |
